title: Athyrium mohillense
taxonomy: Athyrium mohillense is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Athyrium within the family Woodsiaceae. The species is further classified into the order Polypodiales, and then to the class Polypodiopsida. The order Polypodiales is part of the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Further classification of Athyrium mohillense places it within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Athyrium mohillense has been traditionally used in folk medicine. However, its actual medicinal uses are limited in scientific research. There is limited information available on its medicinal properties. More research is needed to understand its potential health benefits. Its traditional uses may vary depending on the region and cultural context.

intro: Athyrium mohillense is a species of fern native to Ireland, belonging to the family Athyriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium mohillense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Athyrium mohillense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in teas and tinctures to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
